title: The Most Recent Three Orders
description: Find the three most recent orders for each customer.
constraints: order_id is the primary key of Orders
customer_id is the primary key of Customers
A customer with fewer than 3 orders — return all their orders
No two orders for the same customer share the same order_date
Result ordered by customer_name ASC, customer_id ASC, order_date DESC
key_concept: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date DESC) <= 3.

hints.L1: Assign row numbers per customer ordered by date descending. Keep rows 1, 2, 3.
hints.L2: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date DESC) AS rn FROM Orders) SELECT ... WHERE rn <= 3
hints.L3: SELECT c.name AS customer_name, o.customer_id, o.order_id, o.order_date FROM (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date DESC) AS rn FROM Orders) o JOIN Customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.rn <= 3

optimal_solution:
SELECT c.name AS customer_name, o.customer_id, o.order_id, o.order_date
FROM (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date DESC) AS rn FROM Orders) o
JOIN Customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE o.rn <= 3 ORDER BY customer_name, o.customer_id, o.order_date DESC;
